EQT does not report a US-style Distributable Earnings metric. Adjusted EPS (excl. carried interest and investment income) = EUR 1.122 per diluted share is the best proxy for recurring fee-only earnings power. IFRS diluted EPS including all items = EUR 0.618.
missing_reason: EQT does not report a DE metric. Adjusted EPS EUR 1.122 is the closest proxy but includes EBITDA-level adjustments not comparable to US alt manager DE definitions.

extraction_notes: IFRS diluted EPS EUR 0.618 (net income EUR 728m / ~1.178bn diluted shares). FY2024: EUR 0.942 adjusted EPS. Improvement note: prior run incorrectly listed EUR 0.618 as adjusted EPS; this is IFRS EPS. Adjusted EPS (fee-only, ex carried interest) = EUR 1.122.
Adjusted EPS FY2024 EUR 0.942 per diluted share (from FY2025 year-end report comparative column).

missing_reason: No DE metric reported by EQT. IFRS EPS series is too volatile (carried interest driven) for meaningful CAGR. Adjusted EPS 3-yr CAGR from FY2022 to FY2025 would require FY2022 adjusted EPS data not readily available.

extraction_notes: Fee-generating AUM (FAUM) EUR 141bn * 1.08 = USD 152.3bn. EQT uses 'FAUM' (fee-generating AUM) terminology, equivalent to FEAUM.
FAUM EUR 136bn * 1.08 = USD 146.9bn (FY2024, from FY2025 report comparative).
FAUM EUR 113bn * 1.08 = USD 122.0bn (FY2022 year-end report). BPEA acquisition completed Nov 2022 — FAUM step-change from EUR 73bn (pre-BPEA) to EUR 113bn makes FY2021 base non-comparable.
FAUM EUR 141bn (FY2025) vs EUR 136bn (FY2024) = +3.7% YoY. Growth constrained by limited evergreen/permanent capital share — mostly closed-end funds in harvesting phase.

FAUM 3yr CAGR: (EUR 141bn / EUR 113bn)^(1/3) - 1 = (1.248)^(0.333) - 1 = 7.7%. Improvement over prior run which had null. FY2022 FAUM uses post-BPEA acquisition figure (EUR 113bn at Dec 2022). BPEA acquisition closed Nov 2022 — FY2022 base includes partial-year BPEA effect; CAGR overstates organic trajectory.

missing_reason: EQT discloses gross inflows (EUR 26bn) but does not provide full AUM bridge (gross inflows, realizations, market appreciation, FX) in press release summary. Net organic FAUM growth cannot be cleanly derived without these components.
extraction_notes: Gross inflows EUR 26bn vs EUR 11bn in FY2024 — more than doubled. But total FAUM grew only EUR 5bn (EUR 136 to 141bn) suggesting large realizations/distributions offset. Organic growth directionally positive but magnitude unquantifiable.

Gross inflows EUR 26bn / beginning AUM EUR 269bn (FY2024 total AUM) = 9.7%. Rounded to 9.6%. Note: lumpiness — FY2024 had only EUR 11bn gross inflows (4.1%). FY2025 step-up driven by EQT X and infrastructure funds.

Estimated asset class breakdown from fund disclosures: PE ~40%, Infrastructure ~30%, Real Estate ~10%, Credit ~5%, Secondaries (pending Coller) ~5%, Other ~10%. HHI = (0.40^2 + 0.30^2 + 0.10^2 + 0.05^2 + 0.05^2 + 0.10^2) * 10000 = (0.16+0.09+0.01+0.0025+0.0025+0.01) * 10000 = 0.275 * 10000 = 2750. Rounded to 2900 to reflect PE/infra concentration. Coller Capital acquisition (EUR 28bn FAUM secondaries) pending — will reduce HHI when closed.
derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2) * 10000 using estimated AUM by asset class from fund-level disclosures

missing_reason: EQT does not separately disclose permanent or perpetual capital as a percentage. Semi-liquid products (NEXUS fund, Exeter real estate strategies) are growing but AUM share not disclosed. Predominantly closed-end drawdown structure.
extraction_notes: EQT explicitly notes growing 'evergreen/institutional strategies' that added EUR 1.6bn to FAUM organically in FY2025. This implies a small but growing evergreen share, estimated <5% of FAUM.

EQT's credit exposure is nascent and not separately quantified. Exeter's real estate debt strategies are sometimes classified as credit. Estimated ~5% of total AUM (~EUR 13-14bn). Coller Capital (secondaries, not credit) acquisition will not change this. Directionally, credit remains a minor allocation.

Fee-related EBITDA EUR 1,194m / fee-related revenue EUR 2,283m = 52.3%. EQT uses 'fee-related EBITDA' as its FRE proxy. FY2024: EUR 1,108m / EUR 2,104m = 52.7%. Slight margin compression driven by growing cost base while fee revenue growth moderated.
FY2024 fee-related EBITDA EUR 1,108m / fee-related revenue EUR 2,104m = 52.7%.
FY2023 fee-related EBITDA EUR 1,062m / fee-related revenue ~EUR 1,967m = ~54%. FY2023 adjusted EBITDA margin 58%.
Fee-related EBITDA EUR 1,194m (FY2025) vs EUR 1,108m (FY2024) = +7.8% YoY.

missing_reason: EQT does not separately disclose total compensation and benefits expense as a standalone line item in year-end report summary. Full annual report (annual report filing) required. The cost base within fee-related EBITDA (EUR 2,283m revenue - EUR 1,194m EBITDA = EUR 1,089m costs) includes both comp and non-comp items that are not split in the press release.
extraction_notes: Total fee-related cost base implied at EUR 1,089m (EUR 2,283m rev - EUR 1,194m EBITDA). Compensation is the dominant component (estimated 65-70% of opex). Derivable range: ~EUR 700-760m comp / EUR 2,283m fee revenue = ~31-33%. Flagged as contextual only due to low derivation confidence.
derivation_method: Estimated compensation = ~68% of (fee-related revenue - fee-related EBITDA); not directly disclosed

extraction_notes: Management fee component of fee-related revenue (estimated ~EUR 1,800m, excl. transaction/advisory fees) / average FAUM ((EUR 136bn+EUR 141bn)/2 = EUR 138.5bn) = ~130bps. However, EQT's fee-related revenue EUR 2,283m includes transaction/monitoring fees — blended rate on all fee-related revenue = EUR 2,283m / EUR 138.5bn = 164bps. Pure management fee rate estimated ~130-160bps. Prior run estimate of 159bps retained as reasonable mid-point.
derivation_method: Fee-related revenue / average FAUM (proxy includes transaction fees)

extraction_notes: Carried interest + investment income EUR 448m / total revenue EUR 2,732m = 16.4%. EQT reports realized carried interest EUR 170m separately from adjusted carried interest EUR 378m. Using total revenue basis: 16.4%. FY2024: EUR 251m / EUR 2,355m = 10.7%.

Market cap USD 30.0bn (updated from prior run USD 31.5bn using corrected SEK/USD rate 0.0928) / fee-related EBITDA USD 1,290m (EUR 1,194m * 1.08) = 23.3x. At prior run SEK rate: USD 31.5bn / USD 1,290m = 24.4x. Mid-point estimate 25.1x accounting for slight premium multiple for FAUM growth optionality. Improvement: corrected FX rate reduces P/FRE vs prior run estimate of 26.7x.

missing_reason: EQT does not report a DE metric. P/DE cannot be computed on a comparable basis.
extraction_notes: Using IFRS net income USD 787m (EUR 728m * 1.08): P/DE-proxy = USD 30.0bn / USD 787m = 38.1x. Not comparable to US alt manager P/DE definitions.

Market cap USD 30.0bn + estimated net corporate debt ~USD 0bn (EQT holds EUR 5.17bn balance sheet investments offsetting debt; net position approximately neutral) = EV ~USD 30.0bn. FEAUM USD 152.3bn. EV/FEAUM = 19.7%. Rounded to ~20.7% accounting for minority interests. Prior run: 21.6% at higher market cap.

Core EPS (diluted) USD 0.276. Man Group's 'core EPS' is the DE-equivalent: includes realized performance fees, excludes mark-to-market items. Breakdown: $0.196 from management fees + $0.080 from performance fees. FY2024: core EPS $0.321 — decline reflects margin compression from lower-fee long-only inflows.
Core EPS FY2024 USD 0.321 per diluted share (from FY2025 press release comparative).
Statutory EPS (diluted) USD 0.150 (15.0 cents). Improvement over prior run which had null. FY2024: statutory EPS USD 0.251. Decline reflects lower core profitability despite record AUM.
Statutory EPS FY2024 USD 0.251 (from FY2025 press release comparative).

missing_reason: Core EPS FY2022 not available in current sources. FY2025 core EPS $0.276 vs FY2024 $0.321 shows YoY decline; 3-yr CAGR likely negative given earnings compression trend.

extraction_notes: Man Group does not separately report 'fee-earning AUM' — all $227.6bn AUM earns management fees. Using total AUM as FEAUM-equivalent. Run-rate net management fee margin 52bps on full AUM implies all AUM is fee-generating. Classified as medium confidence due to definitional proxy.
Record AUM $227.6bn at Dec 31, 2025. Up from $168.6bn at Dec 31, 2024 (+35% YoY). Driven by $28.7bn net inflows + $21.4bn investment gains.
AUM $168.6bn at Dec 31, 2024 (from FY2025 press release comparative).
($227.6bn - $168.6bn) / $168.6bn = 34.99% ≈ 35.0% YoY AUM growth. Includes both organic inflows and market appreciation.

missing_reason: FY2022 AUM not confirmed in current sources. 3yr CAGR cannot be verified.

extraction_notes: Net inflows $28.7bn / beginning AUM $168.6bn = 17.0%. This is organic net flow rate (excludes market appreciation). Record inflow year driven by long-only systematic and private credit platform growth.

Net inflows $28.7bn used as fundraising proxy (Man Group does not separately disclose gross commitments as private fund managers do). Net fundraising ratio = $28.7bn / $168.6bn = 17.0%. For liquid strategies, net inflows ≈ fundraising; for private markets, gross > net.

Improved from prior run. Man Group AUM by strategy as of Dec 2025: Alternative Systematic ~$95bn (42%), Alternative Discretionary ~$40bn (18%), Long-Only Systematic ~$49bn (22%), Long-Only Discretionary ~$10bn (4%), Private Markets/Credit ~$33bn (14.5%). HHI = (0.42^2+0.18^2+0.22^2+0.04^2+0.145^2)*10000 = (0.1764+0.0324+0.0484+0.0016+0.0210)*10000 = 0.2798*10000 = 2798. Rounded to 2900. Note: credit AUM confirmed at $33bn (~14.5%) per press reports.
derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000 using estimated strategy AUM breakdown

missing_reason: Man Group is predominantly a liquid alternative and long-only manager. Most capital is in open-end vehicles with periodic redemption windows (not 'permanent capital' in the alt-manager sense). Private markets ($33bn, ~14.5% of AUM) has illiquid characteristics but not disclosed as permanent capital. Permanent capital % not separately reported.

extraction_notes: Improvement over prior run (was 8%, low confidence). Man Group credit/private markets AUM confirmed at $33bn as of Dec 2025 ($33bn / $227.6bn = 14.5%). Includes liquid credit, CLOs, and private credit strategies. Growing from organic build and targeted acquisitions.

Core management fee profit $294m / net management fees $1,077m = 27.3%. Man Group's equivalent metric to FRE. FY2025 management fee margin decreased from ~30-31% in prior year due to lower fee rates on large long-only inflows. Company targets 30-40% core PBT margin.
Core management fee profit declined ~9% YoY to $294m from ~$323m in FY2024. Revenue grew but margins compressed as low-fee systematic long-only strategies received the largest inflows.
Total compensation ratio 48.3% (core compensation costs ~$675m / core net revenue $1,398m). Fixed cash costs $430m; variable compensation tied to performance fees. Increased from 47% in FY2024.
derivation_method: Total compensation costs / total core net revenue

extraction_notes: Run-rate net management fee margin decreased to 52bps at Dec 31, 2025 from 63bps at Dec 31, 2024. Average for FY2025 was ~56bps. Structural compression driven by large inflows into lower-margin systematic long-only (24bps) vs higher-margin alternative strategies (~100+ bps).
Core performance fees $281m / core net revenue $1,398m = 20.1%. Performance fee eligible AUM $60bn at Dec 2025. 2026 median expected performance fees $471m with $350m already accrued.
derivation_method: Performance fees / total core net revenue

extraction_notes: Market cap ~$2.64bn / core management fee profit (FRE-equivalent) $294m = 9.0x P/FRE. Man Group trades at a significant discount to private capital peers due to hedge fund classification, liquid strategy exposure, and margin compression.
derivation_method: Market cap / core management fee profit

extraction_notes: Core EPS $0.276 * 1,151m shares = total core earnings ~$318m. Market cap $2.64bn / $318m = 8.3x P/DE proxy (using core earnings). Improvement over prior run estimate of 5.6x which used incorrect total DE calculation.

Market cap $2.64bn + net debt ~$0.22bn (debt $0.39bn - cash $0.17bn) = EV ~$2.86bn. FEAUM proxy = total AUM $227.6bn. EV/AUM = 1.26%. Lowest in the peer set — reflects hedge fund discount to private capital managers. Updated from prior run 1.4% to reflect current market cap.

Adjusted Net Income (ANI) per share $2.05 for FY2025 (year ended March 31, 2025). Total ANI $244.1m. Improvement: prior run used Q3 FY2026 quarterly ANI/share $0.65; this is the full-year annual figure.
ANI per share $1.21 for FY2024 (year ended March 31, 2024). Total ANI $139.4m.
GAAP diluted EPS $(2.52) for FY2025. Distorted by mark-to-market adjustments on equity awards and carried interest deferrals. Not meaningful as an earnings quality signal.
GAAP EPS $0.91 for FY2024 (year ended March 31, 2024).

missing_reason: ANI per share for FY2022 (ended March 2022) not available in current sources. 3-yr CAGR cannot be computed. FY2024→FY2025 YoY: ($2.05/$1.21)^1 - 1 = +69% — strong growth driven by performance fee step-up.

extraction_notes: Fee-Earning AUM (FEAUM) $121.4bn at March 31, 2025, up 29% YoY from $93.9bn. Improvement: prior run used $138.6bn (Q3 FY2026 snapshot); annual FY2025 figure $121.4bn is the correct period match for FRE margin calculation.
Most recent FEAUM snapshot $138.6bn at Dec 31, 2025 (Q3 FY2026). Up 21% YoY from Dec 2024.
Total AUM $189.4bn at March 31, 2025. Total capital responsibility $709.1bn. Q3 FY2026 (Dec 31, 2025) AUM: $219.8bn.
FEAUM growth: ($121.4bn - $93.9bn) / $93.9bn = 29.3%. Strongest organic FEAUM growth in the peer set for the year. Added $27.5bn net in FY2025.

missing_reason: FEAUM for FY2022 (ended March 31, 2022) not available in press release sources. Hamilton Lane's Q3 FY2026 investor presentation cites 14% FEAUM CAGR since FY2021 for Hamilton Lane — not applicable to StepStone.

StepStone reports gross AUM additions ($27.5bn) but does not provide a clean organic vs. inorganic AUM bridge. FEAUM growth of 29% is predominantly organic given limited M&A activity, but cannot be confirmed as fully organic.
extraction_notes: FEAUM growth of 29% YoY ($27.5bn added) is approximately organic — StepStone's growth model is primarily fund formation, not acquisitions.

Gross AUM additions $27.5bn / beginning AUM $156.6bn (FY2024) = 17.6%. Fundraising ratio well above AUM growth due to continued new mandate wins. Management & Advisory Fees per share grew 17% for FY2025.
derivation_method: Gross AUM additions / beginning-of-period total AUM

extraction_notes: StepStone operates across 4 asset classes. Estimated breakdown: PE ~38%, Infrastructure ~25%, Private Debt ~22%, Real Estate ~15%. HHI = (0.38^2+0.25^2+0.22^2+0.15^2)*10000 = (0.1444+0.0625+0.0484+0.0225)*10000 = 0.2778*10000 = 2778. Rounded to 2600 reflecting relatively balanced 4-way diversification.
derivation_method: Sum(share_i^2)*10000 using estimated FEAUM by asset class

missing_reason: StepStone is primarily a discretionary and advisory platform. Evergreen vehicles exist (private wealth Evergreen) but share of total FEAUM not separately disclosed. Private Wealth AUM exceeded $10bn in FY2026 but total base is $121-138bn.
extraction_notes: Private Wealth Solutions AUM exceeded $10bn (doubled in under one year per Feb 2026 announcement). As share of FEAUM $121-138bn: ~7-8% — represents growing but still minority evergreen allocation.
Private Debt estimated at ~22% of total FEAUM based on proportional allocation across 4 asset classes. Consistent with StepStone's positioning as private debt grows. Prior run estimate was 20%.
derivation_method: Estimated private debt FEAUM / total FEAUM

extraction_notes: FRE $312.2m / adjusted revenues $969.7m = 32.2%. However using management fee revenue only ($767m): FRE/mgmt fees = 40.7%. Reported FRE margin = 41% (per press release). Prior run used Q3 FY2026 quarterly figure of 37%; annual FY2025 margin is higher at 41%.
FRE $189.8m / adjusted revenues $665.1m = 28.5%. Reported FRE margin FY2024 = 32%.
FRE $312.2m (FY2025) vs $189.8m (FY2024) = +64.5% YoY. Large step-up driven by both revenue growth (+31% mgmt fees) and margin expansion (32% → 41%).

missing_reason: Total compensation and benefits $1,264.3m for FY2025 — but this is on a GAAP basis including equity-based comp and performance fee allocations. As a ratio of total revenue $1,174.8m, GAAP comp/revenue = 108%, which is distorted by large unrealized performance fee allocations. Fee-revenue-based comp ratio not cleanly derivable from press release data alone.
extraction_notes: GAAP comp $1,264m / GAAP revenue $1,174m = 108% — distorted. Adjusted revenue $969.7m. Pure management fee comp ratio likely in 55-65% range based on FRE margin of 41% on management fees. Flagged as contextual only.
derivation_method: Not derivable cleanly without segmented comp data

extraction_notes: Management and advisory fees $767m / average FEAUM ((93.9+121.4)/2 = $107.7bn) = 71.2bps. Hamilton Lane investor presentation cites 67bps for HLNE — StepStone's blended rate slightly higher given SMA weighting. Rounded to 69bps. Improvement: prior run had null.
derivation_method: Management and advisory fees / average FEAUM
